user_query:
I’m preparing a training program for our field team to address the recent incident where an official publicly dismissed a lawyer’s efforts. Use the local inputs to design a short, data-driven curriculum and draft a volunteer invitation email.

Inputs to use:
- input/comments.csv (community feedback log)
- input/remarks.txt (excerpt and context of the official’s dismissal of the lawyer’s work)

Produce all of the following outputs, using only the local inputs (no Internet):
1) output/stats/topic_summary.csv
   - Columns: topic,total_count,percent_of_all
   - percent_of_all must be the topic’s share of all rows in input/comments.csv, rounded to one decimal.
   - Include every topic present in the input.
2) output/stats/sentiment_summary.json
   - JSON with fields:
     - total_comments: integer
     - counts: object with keys positive, neutral, negative (integers)
     - percentages: object with keys positive, neutral, negative (percent of total, rounded to one decimal)
3) output/curriculum/training_plan.json
   - JSON object with:
     - total_comments: integer (must match the number of rows in input/comments.csv)
     - top_topics: array of the three topics with highest total_count from output/stats/topic_summary.csv; each entry must include {topic, count, percent}.
     - modules: array of module objects, constructed as follows:
       • Create one module for each of the three top_topics (title can be your choice but must clearly map to the topic).
       • Create an additional “Case Study” module that explicitly addresses the official’s dismissal incident; set its topic to "dismissal_response".
       • For each module, include: id (string), title (string), topic (string), duration_minutes (integer), learning_objectives (array of 3–5 short strings), content_outline (array of 4–8 bullet points), references (object with keys: comments_source: "input/comments.csv", official_dismissal_excerpt: a 1–2 sentence excerpt from input/remarks.txt).
       • Duration rule for each module: duration_minutes = 20 + (5 × count_of_comments_for_that_module’s_topic) where count_of_comments_for_that_module’s_topic is the number of rows in input/comments.csv whose topic matches the module’s topic.
4) output/messaging/volunteer_invite.txt
   - A plain-text email to volunteers that includes:
     - A subject line starting with: "Training Invite:"
     - A 2–3 sentence opening referencing our commitment to accountability and the incident (drawn from input/remarks.txt).
     - A short summary of the top_topics: list each topic with its count and percent_of_all (from output/stats/topic_summary.csv).
     - A simple schedule outline listing each module title with its duration_minutes (from output/curriculum/training_plan.json).
     - A clear call to RSVP by replying to the email.

Verification notes:
- The value of total_comments must be consistent across sentiment_summary.json and training_plan.json and equal the number of data rows in input/comments.csv.
- The module durations must reflect the stated formula and the topic counts derived from input/comments.csv.
- The Case Study module must include a direct excerpt from input/remarks.txt in references. Write all outputs to the exact paths specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
